nodes.task:
Fix pi readiness detection so spawning a pi agent doesn't appear hung for 60s.

Root cause: harness-ts returns 'dialog' on pi's 'Update Available' banner, but pi.handleDialog returns null (banner is non-blocking, no keys to dismiss it). waitForReady loops until the 60s timeout. Pi's actual idle prompt is also not recognized — it shows a horizontal-rule frame around an empty cursor row, then a footer like '~/cwd (branch)' followed by a cost+token line ending '... (sub) X.X%/Yk (auto)' and a model name (e.g. 'gpt-5.5 • medium').

Files to MODIFY:
  - src/adapters/pi.ts
  - tests/unit/adapters/pi.test.ts

TDD order:

1) WRITE FAILING TESTS in tests/unit/adapters/pi.test.ts. Add three new cases (do not delete the existing five; they must keep passing):
   (a) 'returns ready when Update Available banner sits above pi idle prompt' — pane contains BOTH the 'Update Available' banner text AND a footer line containing '(sub)' and '%/' and '(auto)'. Expect detectReady -> 'ready'.
   (b) 'returns non-ready when banner is present but no idle prompt yet' — pane contains 'Update Available' but no '(sub) ... (auto)' footer line. Expect detectReady -> 'loading' OR 'dialog' (anything that is NOT 'ready'). Use expect(state).not.toBe('ready').
   (c) 'returns ready for pi idle prompt without banner' — pane contains only the footer pattern '~/cwd (branch)\n... (sub) 12.3%/200k (auto)\ngpt-5.5 • medium' (no banner). Expect detectReady -> 'ready'.
   You may synthesize realistic pane samples from the patterns above. tests/fixtures/session-logs/pi/session.jsonl is sparse — do not rely on it.
   Run: bun test tests/unit/adapters/pi.test.ts — confirm new tests fail.

2) IMPLEMENT in src/adapters/pi.ts:
   - Import stripAnsi from '../utils/stripAnsi' if not already imported.
   - Override detectReady locally: first call upstream `harness.detectReady?.(pane) ?? 'loading'` to get the upstream signal. Then post-process: if upstream is 'dialog' OR 'loading', strip ansi from `pane` and look for pi's footer pattern. Use a regex that matches the cost-stats line (the '(sub)' segment is the most reliable marker) — e.g. /\(sub\)\s+\d+(?:\.\d+)?%\/\d+k\s+\(auto\)/. If the regex matches, return 'ready'. Otherwise return the upstream signal unchanged.
   - Do not change spawnArgs, handleDialog, or detectStatus.

3) Re-run: bun test tests/unit/adapters/pi.test.ts — must be GREEN (8 cases total).

4) Sanity: bunx tsc --noEmit (pre-existing baseline errors are not regressions; only new errors are blocking).

No 'as any' / 'as unknown as' casts. No Co-Authored-By trailers. Do not write a coder.md to FLT_RUN_DIR — your handoff goes in <worktree>/coder.md per the standard role contract.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Add defense-in-depth to spawn.ts:waitForReady so any adapter (not just pi) returns within ~10s when its detector is blind to the agent being up.

Files to MODIFY:
  - src/commands/spawn.ts (function waitForReady, ~line 393-437)
Files to CREATE:
  - tests/unit/spawn-wait-for-ready.test.ts

The two changes (BOTH must land):
  (A) When detectReady returns 'dialog' AND adapter.handleDialog(pane) returns null (no keys), STOP looping in the dialog branch — fall through and treat the pane as if 'loading' for stable-counter purposes. Don't continue forever waiting for keys that will never come.
  (B) Add a stable-pane fallback counter: increment whenever pane === lastContent regardless of detectReady's state. After ~8 seconds (16 polls at 500ms) of unchanged content, return early as if ready. Add a one-line comment noting this is the timeout-fast-path: agent is up, detector is just blind to it.

Refactor for testability: extract waitForReady's tmux interaction so the test can inject a fake. Suggested signature:
  async function waitForReady(
    session: string,
    adapter: Pick<CliAdapter, 'detectReady' | 'handleDialog'>,
    timeoutMs = 30_000,
    deps: { capturePane: (s: string) => string; sendKeys: (s: string, keys: string[]) => void; hasSession: (s: string) => boolean; sleep: (ms: number) => Promise<void> } = { capturePane: tmux.capturePane, sendKeys: tmux.sendKeys, hasSession: tmux.hasSession, sleep },
  ): Promise<void>
All existing call sites must continue to work — leave the deps parameter optional with the existing tmux/sleep defaults. Export waitForReady (named export) so the test can import it; if exporting requires moving it above its callers, do that.

TDD order:

1) WRITE FAILING TESTS in tests/unit/spawn-wait-for-ready.test.ts using bun:test:
   - Case 1 'returns within ~10s when adapter reports dialog with no keys': stub adapter.detectReady -> 'dialog', adapter.handleDialog -> null. Stub capturePane to return the same string each call. Pass deps with sleep set to a fake that resolves immediately (so the test runs fast) but use Date.now() readings via a clock-stub OR rely on counting iterations: assert waitForReady resolves AND that the loop exited via the stable-pane fallback (e.g. by counting capturePane calls — should be ~16, not >100).
   - Case 2 'returns on first ready+stable pane (no regression)': adapter.detectReady returns 'ready' every call, capturePane returns the same string. Assert it resolves after 2 stable reads (the existing semantics) — capturePane call count should be small (e.g. <= 4).
   - Case 3 'returns when session dies': stub hasSession to return false on the first call. Assert it throws Error matching /died during startup/.
   Inject a fake sleep that resolves immediately (Promise.resolve()) — do NOT actually sleep 500ms 16 times in a unit test. Do NOT touch tmux for real.
   Run: bun test tests/unit/spawn-wait-for-ready.test.ts — confirm cases 1 and (depending on implementation) 2 fail before changes.

2) IMPLEMENT both (A) and (B) in src/commands/spawn.ts. Plus the deps-injection refactor described above. Keep the existing default behavior intact for production callers.

3) Re-run: bun test tests/unit/spawn-wait-for-ready.test.ts — GREEN. Also re-run tests/unit/spawn.test.ts and tests/unit/spawn-workdir-safety.test.ts to confirm no regression.

4) bunx tsc --noEmit. No new errors.

No 'as any' / 'as unknown as' casts. No Co-Authored-By trailers.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Replace the single-agent 'mutate' step in templates/workflows/daily-mutator.yaml with a parallel fan-out that produces one candidate per role-category. YAML-only change. NO new tests.

File to MODIFY:
  - templates/workflows/daily-mutator.yaml

Keep collect, redact, find_skills, eval, and gate steps EXACTLY as they are. Only the 'mutate' step changes.

The new 'mutate' step must be a parallel step with this exact shape (preserve indentation, preserve id/on_complete/on_fail wiring):

  - id: mutate
    type: parallel
    n: 6
    presets: [cc-mutator, cc-mutator, cc-mutator, cc-mutator, cc-mutator, cc-mutator]
    step:
      id: mutate-child
      preset: cc-mutator
      worktree: true
      task: |
        You are a per-domain GEPA mutator. Map your $FLT_RUN_LABEL to one role file:
          a -> templates/roles/coder.md
          b -> templates/roles/reviewer.md
          c -> templates/roles/architect.md
          d -> templates/roles/oracle.md
          e -> templates/roles/mutator.md
          f -> templates/roles/evaluator.md
        If your label is anything else, flt workflow fail "unmapped label: $FLT_RUN_LABEL".
        Inputs:
        - $FLT_RUN_DIR/artifacts/trace-bundle-redacted.txt
        - $FLT_RUN_DIR/artifacts/skill-candidates.txt
        - Focus area: {task}
        Required outputs in $FLT_RUN_DIR/artifacts/ (label-scoped, must not collide):
        1) <role>-$FLT_RUN_LABEL.vNext.md with YAML front-matter (stable_path absolute, mutation_type: prompt_edit | skill_acquisition), then full proposed body.
        2) <role>-$FLT_RUN_LABEL.hypothesis.json shape: { artifact, mutation_type, hypothesis, expected_delta }.
        Rules: one candidate per child; stable_path absolute; do not edit the stable file in place.
        flt workflow pass when both files exist with label-scoped names.
        flt workflow fail "<reason>" only if you cannot mutate at all.
    on_complete: eval
    on_fail: abort

Verification:
  1) bun test tests/unit/workflow-parser.test.ts — must be GREEN. The fixture in this test already includes 'cc-mutator' so the parse should succeed without further changes.
  2) Also run: bun test tests/unit/init-workflows.test.ts — GREEN.
  3) Spot-check: cat templates/workflows/daily-mutator.yaml — confirm the eval/gate steps below 'mutate' are unchanged from main HEAD (presets: [pi-coder, pi-coder] for eval, etc).

No Co-Authored-By trailers.

flt workflow pass when bun test on the two parser tests is green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Make pending gates visible from the TUI chrome without opening the gates modal.

Two improvements:
  (a) Persistent gate-count badge: when there are >=1 pending gates of any kind (human_gate, node-fail, reconcile-fail, node-candidate, blocker, question), show '(N gate)' or '(N gates)' in the status bar at src/tui/panels.ts:renderStatusBar (~line 641). Append it to the existing summary string after a separator (e.g. `${summary} | ${gateBadge}`). When there are zero pending gates, show NOTHING (no badge, no parentheses).
  (b) Banner-on-new-gate: when the gates-modal watcher detects a NEW gate (count went up since last refresh), call the existing app.setBanner('New gate: <runId> <kind> — press g', 'yellow', 6000). Use the existing setBanner method on the App — do NOT invent a new mechanism.

Files to INSPECT first (do not skip — read them before changing anything):
  - src/tui/panels.ts (renderStatusBar at ~line 641 — where the badge goes; AppState shape — see top of file)
  - src/tui/app.ts (App class, setBanner method ~line 788; gatesModal lifecycle ~line 1447; how gates are scanned)
  - src/tui/modal-gates.ts (loadAllRows, openGatesWatcher — REUSE; do NOT duplicate)
  - src/gates.ts (scanGates, scanBlockers — exposed helpers)
  - src/qna.ts (pendingQna)

Files to MODIFY:
  - src/tui/panels.ts (renderStatusBar + a small helper for the badge text)
  - src/tui/app.ts (compute gate count; track previous count; trigger setBanner on increase)
  - Possibly src/tui/types.ts (if AppState needs a `gateCount: number` and `lastGateCount: number` field)

Files to CREATE:
  - tests/unit/tui-gates-badge.test.ts

Design details:
  - Add a small pure helper exported from panels.ts (or a new src/tui/gates-badge.ts if cleaner): `formatGateBadge(count: number): string` that returns '' for 0, '(1 gate)' for 1, and '(N gates)' for N>=2. This is the unit-testable surface.
  - Renumber pluralization carefully: 1 -> 'gate', else 'gates'.
  - In the App, on every gates-watcher tick (where modal-gates already triggers refresh), compute the total pending count via the existing scanGates() + pendingQna() helpers (the same ones modal-gates.ts uses in loadAllRows — extract a tiny shared helper if needed, or just call both directly). Update state.gateCount and, if the count INCREASED since last tick, call setBanner with the most recently added row's runId+kind. If multiple were added, just use the first new one.
  - The watcher must run regardless of whether the gates modal is open — so the badge updates in real time. Inspect openGatesWatcher and the polling/refresh logic in app.ts to find the right hook. If the existing watcher is only created when the modal opens, add a SEPARATE always-on lightweight poller/watcher in App that updates gateCount; reuse scanGates/pendingQna as the data source. Do NOT duplicate the scan logic — call the existing exports.

TDD order:

1) WRITE FAILING TESTS in tests/unit/tui-gates-badge.test.ts:
   - 'formatGateBadge returns empty for 0 gates' -> expect('').
   - 'formatGateBadge returns (1 gate) for 1' -> expect('(1 gate)').
   - 'formatGateBadge returns (3 gates) for 3' -> expect('(3 gates)').
   - Banner trigger test: if you can isolate the count-rising logic into a pure function (e.g. `maybeTriggerGateBanner(prev: number, curr: number, latestGate: { runId; kind }): { text: string; color: string; durationMs: number } | null`), unit-test it: prev=0,curr=1 -> banner with text matching /New gate.*press g/ and color 'yellow' and duration 6000. prev=1,curr=1 -> null. prev=2,curr=1 -> null (count went DOWN). If extracting that function would require contorting app.ts, instead leave a comment in the test file: 'banner trigger intentionally not unit-tested — exercised manually in the TUI'.
   Run: bun test tests/unit/tui-gates-badge.test.ts — FAIL.

2) IMPLEMENT (a) the formatGateBadge helper + wire it into renderStatusBar; (b) the watcher + setBanner trigger.

3) Re-run: bun test tests/unit/tui-gates-badge.test.ts — GREEN.

4) Re-run all tui tests: bun test tests/unit/tui — GREEN, no regressions.

5) bunx tsc --noEmit — no new errors.

Do NOT spin up the actual screen in tests; the badge test is string-formatting only.
Do NOT duplicate scanGates/pendingQna logic — reuse the existing exports.
No 'as any' / 'as unknown as' casts. No Co-Authored-By trailers.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Add a 'grill' clarification step at the front of two bundled workflows: idea-to-pr and new-project. The grill step uses 'flt ask human' to resolve genuine ambiguities BEFORE spec/research runs. YAML-only change with parser test verification.

Files to MODIFY:
  - templates/workflows/idea-to-pr.yaml
  - templates/workflows/new-project.yaml

Grill step definition (use this body verbatim, both workflows, with cc-sonnet preset — the judgment about ambiguity is light):

  - id: grill
    preset: cc-sonnet
    task: |
      Original task: {task}
      Read the task above. Identify any ambiguities or scope decisions a coder will need clarified to deliver an unambiguous implementation. Common axes: file structure / naming, error-handling level, whether to add tests vs use existing, framework/library choice when multiple are reasonable, edge-case behavior.
      For each genuine ambiguity (skip questions whose answer is obvious from the task), call:
        flt ask human '{"prompt": "<question>", "options": [{"label": "<a>", "description": "..."}, ...]}'
      Capture each answer; write all Q&A to grill-answers.md in your worktree (one ## section per question, plus the user's chosen answer).
      Cap: at most 3 questions. If the task is unambiguous, skip and write 'No clarifications needed.' to grill-answers.md.
      flt workflow pass when grill-answers.md exists.
    on_complete: <existing-first-step-id>

idea-to-pr.yaml changes:
  - Insert the grill step as the first step. Set its on_complete to 'spec' (the existing first step).
  - Modify the existing 'spec' step task body so the spec writer reads BOTH {task} AND {steps.grill.worktree}/grill-answers.md, treating the grill answers as binding (override task ambiguity). Suggested edit: prepend to spec.task:
      'Original task: {task}\n\nClarifications from grill (binding — these override any ambiguity in the task above): read {steps.grill.worktree}/grill-answers.md and treat its answers as authoritative.\n\n'
    Keep the rest of the spec task body intact ('Write a one-page spec.md ...').

new-project.yaml changes:
  - Insert the grill step as the first step. The existing first step is 'research' (id: research). Set grill's on_complete to 'research'.
  - Modify the existing 'research' step's run command to incorporate the grill answers. Since 'research' uses `run: 'flt ask oracle ...'`, add the grill answers into the oracle prompt — change the run to:
      run: 'flt ask oracle "What is the current best-practice stack and gotchas for: {task} (clarifications: $(cat {steps.grill.worktree}/grill-answers.md 2>/dev/null || echo none))"'
  - Also modify the 'spec' step task to read grill-answers.md the same way idea-to-pr does — prepend a binding clarifications line and reference {steps.grill.worktree}/grill-answers.md.

Verification:
  1) bun test tests/unit/workflow-parser.test.ts — GREEN.
  2) bun test tests/unit/init-workflows.test.ts — GREEN. Both yaml files load and validate.
  3) Sanity: head templates/workflows/idea-to-pr.yaml — first step id is 'grill' with on_complete: spec. Same for new-project.yaml with on_complete: research.

No Co-Authored-By trailers.

flt workflow pass when both parser tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.
